---
title: "nitrix.stats.gam"
generated: true
signature-hash: e947076f6a900be4
description: "Mass-univariate generalised additive (mixed) models."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-gam-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-gam/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.gam`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Mass-univariate generalised additive (mixed) models.

[`gam_fit`](nitrix-stats-gam/gam_fit.qmd) fits, per element (voxel / vertex / fixel), a generalised
additive model

$$
g(\mathbb{E}[y]) = X_{\text{parametric}} \beta
    + \sum_k f_k(x_k), \qquad f_k = B_k(x_k)\,\gamma_k
$$

with each smooth $f_k$ a penalised spline (from the sibling basis module)
carrying a roughness penalty $\lambda_k\,\gamma_k^{\top} S_k\,\gamma_k$.
This is the ModelArray `gam` / `mgcv`-style fit; a *generalised additive
mixed model* (GAMM) adds explicit random-effect blocks, which enter as just
more penalty components (a random effect is a ridge penalty), so the same
machinery covers both.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### Two nested loops, one per element

- **Inner** (fixed $\lambda$): penalised iteratively reweighted least
  squares -- the same solver-free weighted normal-equations solve as the
  generalised linear model, with the block penalty
  $S(\lambda) = \sum_k \lambda_k S_k$ added.  Ordinary and weighted least
  squares and the exponential family all reduce to it.
- **Outer** (select $\lambda$): the generalised Fellner-Schall update
  (Wood & Fasiolo, 2017) -- a multiplicative, positivity-preserving
  generalised-REML step

  $$
\lambda_k \leftarrow \lambda_k\,
  \frac{\operatorname{tr}(S_\lambda^{-} S_k) - \operatorname{tr}(V S_k)}
       {\gamma_k^{\top} S_k\,\gamma_k / \phi}
  $$

  that increases the (Laplace) marginal likelihood each iteration.  Because GAM
  smooths occupy disjoint coefficient blocks,
  $\operatorname{tr}(S_\lambda^{-} S_k) = \operatorname{rank}(S_k)/\lambda_k$
  -- no generalised inverse of the summed penalty is needed.  This is the
  operational form of the penalty / variance-component REML equivalence (the GAM
  smoothing parameter is the ratio $\phi / \sigma_b^2$ of a mixed model).

Both loops run a fixed number of iterations (clean under
`jax.vmap` over elements) and every solve avoids the vendor dense
eigensolver, so the whole fit runs on hardware where that solver is unreliable.

### Outputs (modelarray ``gam`` parity)

[`GAMResult`](nitrix-stats-gam/GAMResult.qmd) carries per-element coefficients, selected $\lambda$,
per-smooth **effective degrees of freedom** (the trace of the smooth's influence
block) and total effective degrees of freedom, dispersion, deviance, and the
Bayesian coefficient covariance $V = (X^{\top} W X + S_\lambda)^{-1}$ (for
smooth-term confidence bands and the approximate $F$ / $\chi^2$
tests).  Partial effects are rendered with [`smooth_partial_effect`](nitrix-stats-gam/smooth_partial_effect.qmd).

### References

Wood, S. N. & Fasiolo, M. (2017). A generalized Fellner-Schall method for
smoothing parameter optimization with application to Tweedie location, scale
and shape models. Biometrics, 73(4), 1071-1081.
https://doi.org/10.1111/biom.12666

Wood, S. N. (2017). Generalized Additive Models: An Introduction with R,
2nd ed. Chapman and Hall/CRC.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-gam-symbols}
:::
